children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In an area that the President has declared a major disaster in accordance with , the Secretary may carry out a watershed assessment to identify, to the maximum extent practicable, specific flood risk reduction, hurricane and storm damage reduction, ecosystem restoration, or navigation project recommendations that will help to rehabilitate and improve the resiliency of damaged infrastructure and natural resources to reduce risks to human life and property from future natural disasters.section 5170 of title 42

children.heading: Existing projects
children.label: 2 Existing projects
children.text: A watershed assessment carried out paragraph (1) may identify existing projects being carried out under 1 or more of the authorities referred to in subsection (b)(1).1So in original. Probably should be preceded by “under”.1

children.heading: Duplicate watershed assessments
children.label: 3 Duplicate watershed assessments
children.text: In carrying out a watershed assessment under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall use all existing watershed assessments and related information developed by the Secretary or other Federal, State, or local entities.

children.label: A
children.text: Section 205 of the Flood Control Act of 1948 ().33 U.S.C. 701s

children.label: B
children.text: Section 111 of the River and Harbor Act of 1968 ().33 U.S.C. 426i

children.label: C
children.text: Section 206 of the Water Resources Development Act of 1996 ().33 U.S.C. 2330

children.label: D
children.text: Section 1135 of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 ().33 U.S.C. 2309a

children.label: E
children.text: Section 107 of the River and Harbor Act of 1960 ().33 U.S.C. 577

children.label: F
children.text: ().Section 3 of the Act of August 13, 194633 U.S.C. 426g

children.heading: Annual plan
children.label: 2 Annual plan
children.text: For each project that does not meet the criteria under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall include a recommendation relating to the project in the annual report submitted to Congress by the Secretary in accordance with .section 2282d of this title

children.label: A
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, use all existing information and studies available for the project; and

children.label: B
children.text: not require any element of a study completed for the project prior to the disaster to be repeated.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: c Requirements
children.text: All requirements applicable to a project under the Acts described in subsection (b) shall apply to the project.

children.heading: Limitations on assessments
children.label: d Limitations on assessments
children.text: A watershed assessment under subsection (a) shall be initiated not later than 2 years after the date on which the major disaster declaration is issued.

children.heading: Assessments in territories of the United States
children.label: e Assessments in territories of the United States

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For any major disaster declared in a territory of the United States before , all activities in the territory carried out or undertaken pursuant to the authorities described in this section shall be conducted at full Federal expense unless the President determines that the territory has the ability to pay the cost share for an assessment under this section without the use of loans.October 23, 2018

children.heading: Territory defined
children.label: 2 Territory defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “territory of the United States” means an insular area specified in .section 2310(a)(1) of this title
